title: Chennai Metro: Route, Map, Timings, Fare and Latest Updates
intro: The Chennai Metro has made daily commuting easier across the city. Understanding metro routes, fares, timings and upcoming projects helps travellers plan smooth and efficient journeys.

sections.content: Chennai Metro is a rapid transit system serving Tamil Nadu’s capital city. Launched in 2015, it is India’s fourth-longest metro network and is operated by Chennai Metro Rail Corporation (CMRL).

items: Operational Lines: Blue Line, Green Line
Under Construction Lines: Purple Line, Orange Line, Red Line
Blue Line (Line 1): Wimco Nagar Depot to Chennai International Airport
Green Line (Line 2): Chennai Central to St. Thomas Mount

sections.content: The Blue Line runs from Wimco Nagar Depot to Chennai International Airport and has 26 stations. Thirteen stations are underground and thirteen are elevated, offering seamless airport connectivity.
The Green Line connects Chennai Central with St. Thomas Mount and has 17 stations. It links central, western and southern parts of Chennai and integrates with MRTS and suburban railways.

items: Purple Line (Line 3): Madhavaram Milk Colony to Siruseri SIPCOT 2
Orange Line (Line 4): Poonamallee Bypass to Lighthouse
Red Line (Line 5): Madhavaram Milk Colony to Sholinganallur

sections.content: Metro services operate daily from 4:30 AM to 11:00 PM. During peak hours, trains run every 4–5 minutes, while during non-peak hours the frequency is around 15 minutes.
Metro fares range between Rs 10 and Rs 50 depending on the distance travelled. Chennai Metro offers affordable ticketing options for daily commuters.

items: Smart Value Cards (20% discount on fares)
Tourist Cards for unlimited one-day travel
Singara Chennai Card under NCMC scheme

sections.content: Phase 2 of the Chennai Metro is under development with major funding from the Tamil Nadu government. Several corridors are nearing completion and services are expected to start in phases by 2027–2028.
Impact of Chennai Metro on Real Estate
The Chennai Metro has significantly boosted real estate growth along metro corridors. Property prices in areas such as Guindy, Anna Nagar, Vadapalani and T. Nagar have increased by 20–40%.

items: Metro projects planned for Trichy, Coimbatore and Madurai
Proposed Semi High-Speed Rail Corridors across Tamil Nadu
Chennai Water Metro project inspired by Kochi Water Metro

sections.content: Chennai Metro continues to transform urban mobility by reducing travel time and improving connectivity. With multiple expansions underway, the metro system is set to play a major role in Chennai’s future development.
